name: check-shapes

metadata.name: check-shapes
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: A library for annotating and checking the shapes of tensors.

documentation:
# check_shapes

`check_shapes` is a library for annotating and checking tensor shapes.
For example:

```python
import tensorflow as tf

from gpflow.experimental.check_shapes import check_shapes

@tf.function
@check_shapes(
    "features: [batch..., n_features]",
    "weights: [n_features]",
    "return: [batch...]",
)
def linear_model(features: tf.Tensor, weights: tf.Tensor) -> tf.Tensor:
    return tf.einsum("...i,i -> ...", features, weights)
```

For more information see our [documentation](https://gpflow.github.io/check_shapes).

## Installation

The recommended way to install `check_shapes` is from pypi:

```bash
pip install check_shapes
```

### From source

To develop `check_shapes`, check it out from GitHub:

```bash
git clone git@github.com:GPflow/check_shapes.git
```

We use [Poetry](https://python-poetry.org/) to install and manage dependencies. Follow their
instructions for how to install Poetry itself. Then:

```bash
cd check_shapes
poetry install
```

To check you installation run our tests:

```bash
poetry run task test
```

For testing with different versions of Python and dependencies, see the `poetryenv` script.

functions.name: check_shapes.checker_context.set_shape_checker
documentation: Sets the current shape checker in the context.

functions.name: check_shapes.checker_context.get_shape_checker
documentation:
Get the {class}`ShapeChecker` from the wrapping {func}`check_shapes` decorator.

Behaviour is undefined if you call this from a function that is not directly wrapped in
{func}`check_shapes` or {func}`inherit_check_shapes`.

functions.name: check_shapes.checker_context.check_shape
documentation:
Raise an error if a tensor has the wrong shape.

This uses the {class}`ShapeChecker` from the wrapping {func}`check_shapes` decorator. Behaviour
is undefined if you call this from a function that is not directly wrapped in
{func}`check_shapes` or {func}`inherit_check_shapes`.

Example:

```{literalinclude} /examples/test_check_shapes_examples.py
:dedent: true
:end-before: '[intermediate_results]'
:start-after: '[intermediate_results]'
```

```
param shaped

The object whose shape to check.

param tensor_spec

Specification to check the tensor against. See "Shape specification" in our
User Guide.

param context

Information about where shaped is coming from, for improved error
messages.

returns

shaped, for convenience.
```

functions.name: check_shapes.shapes.register_get_shape
documentation:
Register a function for extracting the shape from a given type of objects.

Example:

```{literalinclude} /examples/test_check_shapes_examples.py
:dedent: true
:end-before: '[custom_type]'
:start-after: '[custom_type]'
```

See also {func}`get_shape`.

```
param shape_type

Type of objects to extract shapes from.
```

functions.name: check_shapes.shapes.get_shape
documentation:
Returns the shape of the given object.

See also {func}`register_get_shape`.

```
param shaped

The objects whose shape to extract.

param context

Context we are getting the shape in, for improved error messages.

returns

The shape of shaped, or None if the shape exists, but is unknown.

raises NoShapeError

If objects of this type does not have shapes.
```

The `__set_name__` hack is to get access to the class the method was declared on.

functions.name: check_shapes.decorator.add_wrapper_post_processor
documentation: Add a hook for updating / modifying decorated / wrapped functions.

functions.name: check_shapes.decorator.null_check_shapes
documentation:
Annotates the given function so that it looks like it has shape checks, but without actually
checking anything.

This is necessary not to break `@inherit_check_shapes` when shape checking is disabled.

functions.name: check_shapes.decorator.check_shapes
documentation:
Decorator that checks the shapes of tensor arguments.

Example:

```{literalinclude} /examples/test_check_shapes_examples.py
:dedent: true
:end-before: '[basic]'
:start-after: '[basic]'
```

```
param specs

Specification of arguments to check. See: "Check specification" in our User Guide.

param tf_decorator

Whether to wrap the shape check with
tf.compat.v1.flags.tf_decorator.make_decorator.
Setting this  seems to solve some problems, particularly related to Keras models,
but create some other problems, particularly related to branching on tensors.
```

classes.name: check_shapes.decorator.WrapperPostProcessor

methods.name: check_shapes.decorator.WrapperPostProcessor.on_wrap
documentation:
Called whenever `check_shapes` wraps a function.

```
param func

Orginal function, before wrapping.

param wrapped

The wrapper around func, that implements the shape checking.

param signature

The  of func.

param tf_decorator

Whether to apply custom TensorFlow decorator logic. Most users should
ignore this.

return

wrapped or a new wrapper.
```
Hook for updating / modifying decorated / wrapped functions.

modules.name: check_shapes.error_contexts
documentation:
Infrastructure for describing the context of an error.

The {class}`MessageBuilder` is used to format / indent messages nicely.

The {class}`ErrorContext` is a reusable bit of information about where/why an error occurred that
can be written to a {class}`MessageBuilder`.

{class}`ErrorContext`s can be composed using the {class}`StackContext` and
{class}`ParallelContext`.

This allows reusable error messages in a consistent format.
Copyright 2022 The GPflow Contributors. All Rights Reserved.
Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at
http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
pylint: disable=broad-except

variables.name: check_shapes.error_contexts._NO_VALUE
documentation: Sentinel to represent "no value" in places where `None` is a valid value.

classes.name: check_shapes.error_contexts.MessageBuilder

methods.name: check_shapes.error_contexts.MessageBuilder.add_line
documentation:
Write a line, indented at the current level.

Input is converted to a `str` using `str(text)`.

methods.name: check_shapes.error_contexts.MessageBuilder.add_columned_line
documentation:
Write a line of several values, left-aligned within the current indentation level.

Inputs are converted to `str`s using `str(text)`.

methods.name: check_shapes.error_contexts.MessageBuilder.indent
documentation: Context manager for indenting text.

methods.name: check_shapes.error_contexts.MessageBuilder.build
documentation: Compile all collected text into a single string.
Utility for formatting nested text.

classes.name: check_shapes.error_contexts.ErrorContext

methods.name: check_shapes.error_contexts.ErrorContext.print
documentation: Print this context to the given `MessageBuilder`.

A context in which an error can occur.

Contexts should be immutable, and implement {func}`__eq__` - so that they can be composed using
{class}`StackContext` and {class}`ParallelContext`.

The contexts are often created even if an error doesn't actually occur, so they should be cheap
to create - prefer to do any slow computation in {func}`print`, rather than in {func}`__init__`.

Maybe think of an {class}`ErrorContext` as a factory of error messages.

classes.name: check_shapes.error_contexts.StackContext
documentation: Error context where one context is "inside" another one.

classes.name: check_shapes.error_contexts.ParallelContext
documentation: Error context with many contexts in parallel.

classes.name: check_shapes.error_contexts.FunctionCallContext

methods.name: check_shapes.error_contexts.FunctionCallContext.precompute
documentation:
Precompute the values to print.

This is useful to capture the position of the stack of the relevant call, if this object is
saved for later use.

:returns: A new instance with precomptued values.

An error occured inside a function that was called.

Normally `print` should be called from within the called function: `func`.
If that is impossible, first call `precompute` from within `func`.

classes.name: check_shapes.error_contexts.FunctionDefinitionContext
documentation: An error occured in the context of a function definition.

classes.name: check_shapes.error_contexts.VariableContext
documentation: An error occurred in the context of a shape specification variable.

classes.name: check_shapes.error_contexts.TensorSpecContext

documentation: An error occurred in the context of an argument to a function.

classes.name: check_shapes.error_contexts.AttributeContext

classes.name: check_shapes.error_contexts.IndexContext

classes.name: check_shapes.error_contexts.MappingValueContext
documentation: An error occurent in the context of a value in a map.

classes.name: check_shapes.error_contexts.ConditionContext
documentation: An error occurred in a conditional context.

classes.name: check_shapes.error_contexts.ShapeContext
documentation: An error occurred in the context of the shapes of function arguments.

classes.name: check_shapes.error_contexts.NoteContext
documentation: An error occurred in a context where a user has added a note.

classes.name: check_shapes.error_contexts.ObjectValueContext
documentation: An error was caused by the value of an object.

classes.name: check_shapes.error_contexts.ObjectTypeContext
documentation: An error was caused by the type of an object.

classes.name: check_shapes.error_contexts.ParserInputContext
documentation: Abstract base class for contexts that relate to parser input.

classes.name: check_shapes.error_contexts.LarkUnexpectedInputContext
documentation: An error was caused by an `UnexpectedInput` error from `Lark`.

classes.name: check_shapes.error_contexts.MultipleElementBoolContext
documentation: An error was caused by trying to use a multi-element argument specification as a bool.

variables.name: check_shapes.base_types.Dimension
documentation:
The size of a single observed dimension.

Use `None` if the size of that dimension is unknown.

variables.name: check_shapes.base_types.Shape
documentation:
The complete shape of an observed object.

Use `None` if the object has a shape, but the shape is unknown.

Raise an exception if objects of that type can never have a shape.

functions.name: check_shapes.config.add_is_compiled_mode
documentation:
Add a function for determining whether we are currently executing "compiled mode".

Used when func:`set_enable_check_shapes` is set to `EAGER_MODE_ONLY`.

functions.name: check_shapes.config.set_enable_check_shapes
documentation:
Set whether to enable {mod}`check_shapes`.

Check shapes has a non-zero impact on performance. If this is unacceptable to you, you can
use this function to disable it.

Example:

```{literalinclude} /examples/test_check_shapes_examples.py
:dedent: true
:end-before: '[disable__manual]'
:start-after: '[disable__manual]'
```

See also {func}`disable_check_shapes`.

functions.name: check_shapes.config.get_enable_check_shapes
documentation: Get whether to enable {mod}`check_shapes`.

functions.name: check_shapes.config.disable_check_shapes
documentation:
Context manager that temporarily disables shape checking.

Example:

```{literalinclude} /examples/test_check_shapes_examples.py
:dedent: true
:end-before: '[disable__context_manager]'
:start-after: '[disable__context_manager]'
```

functions.name: check_shapes.config.set_rewrite_docstrings
documentation:
Set how {mod}`check_shapes` should rewrite docstrings.

See {class}`DocstringFormat` for valid choices.

functions.name: check_shapes.config.get_rewrite_docstrings
documentation: Get how {mod}`check_shapes` should rewrite docstrings.

functions.name: check_shapes.config.set_enable_function_call_precompute
documentation:
Set whether to precompute function call path and line numbers for debugging.

This is disabled by default, because it is (relatively) slow. Enabling this can give better
error messages.

Example:

```{literalinclude} /examples/test_check_shapes_examples.py
:dedent: true
:end-before: '[disable_function_call_precompute]'
:start-after: '[disable_function_call_precompute]'
```

functions.name: check_shapes.config.get_enable_function_call_precompute
documentation: Get whether to precompute function call path and line numbers for debugging.

functions.name: check_shapes.config.set_drop_frames
documentation: Set whether {mod}`check_shapes` should hide itself from exception stack traces.

functions.name: check_shapes.config.get_drop_frames
documentation: Get whether {mod}`check_shapes` should hide itself from exception stack traces.

classes.name: check_shapes.config.ShapeCheckingState

methods.name: check_shapes.config.ShapeCheckingState.__bool__
documentation: Return whether we currently should check shapes.

class_variables.name: check_shapes.config.ShapeCheckingState.ENABLED
documentation: Always check shapes.

class_variables.name: check_shapes.config.ShapeCheckingState.EAGER_MODE_ONLY
documentation: Only check shapes if `tf.inside_function()` is `False`.

class_variables.name: check_shapes.config.ShapeCheckingState.DISABLED
documentation: Never check shapes.
Different states of whether to actually check shapes.

class_variables.name: check_shapes.config.DocstringFormat.NONE
documentation: Do not rewrite docstrings.
Enumeration of supported formats of docstrings.

classes.name: check_shapes.checker._ObservedDim

methods.name: check_shapes.checker._ObservedDim.check_and_update
documentation:
Attempt to merge new data into this observation.

Returns whether the new data is compatible with existing observations. If this method
returns `False` this object may have been left in an invalid state and should not be used
again.
Storage of observed size of a single dimension variable.

classes.name: check_shapes.checker._ObservedDims

methods.name: check_shapes.checker._ObservedDims.check_and_update
documentation:
Attempt to merge new data into this observation.

Returns whether the new data is compatible with existing observations. If this method
returns `False` this object may have been left in an invalid state and should not be used
again.
Storage of observed sizes of a var-rank / batch variable.

classes.name: check_shapes.checker._ShapeCheck

methods.name: check_shapes.checker._ShapeCheck.finished
documentation: Whether this entire check has been performed.

class_variables.name: check_shapes.checker._ShapeCheck.actual
documentation:
Actual observed shape.

Only `actual[actual_begin:actual_end]` is still waiting to be checked. The beginning and end may
already have been checked.

class_variables.name: check_shapes.checker._ShapeCheck.expected
documentation:
Specification to check against.

Only `expected[expected_begin:expected_end]` is still waiting to be checked. The beginning and
end may already have been checked.
A shape check that is waiting to be performed.

classes.name: check_shapes.checker._VariableState

class_variables.name: check_shapes.checker._VariableState.uses
documentation: List of specs where this variable is used.

class_variables.name: check_shapes.checker._VariableState.observed_dim
documentation:
Observed size of this variable, if the variable is rank-1.

Set this `None` if this variable is varrank.

class_variables.name: check_shapes.checker._VariableState.observed_dims
documentation:
Observed shape of this variable, if the variable is varrank.

Set this `None` if this variable is rank-1.

class_variables.name: check_shapes.checker._VariableState.waiting_for_varrank
documentation: Checks that are waiting for the rank of this variable to be determined.
Structure of stuff we need to know about each variable.

classes.name: check_shapes.checker.ShapeChecker

methods.name: check_shapes.checker.ShapeChecker.add_context
documentation:
Add arbirtary context to the shape checker.

This context will be included in any error messages.

```
param context

Context to add to this shape checker.
```

methods.name: check_shapes.checker.ShapeChecker.check_shape
documentation:
Raise an error if a tensor has the wrong shape.

This remembers observed shapes and specifications, so that tensors can be checked for
compatibility across multiple calls, and so that we can provide good error messages.

```
param shaped

The object whose shape to check.

param tensor_spec

Specification to check the tensor against.
Usually this is a str in the format described under "Shape specification" in our
User Guide.
Alternatively this can be a pre-parsed , or an actual
.

param context

Information about where shaped is coming from, for improved error
messages.

returns

shaped, for convenience.
```

methods.name: check_shapes.checker.ShapeChecker.check_shapes
documentation:
Raise an error if any tensor has the wrong shape.

This remembers observed shapes and specifications, so that tensors can be checked for
compatibility across multiple calls, and so that we can provide good error messages.

```
param checks

Checks to perform. The elements can either be (shaped, tensor_spec) or
(shaped, tensor_spec, context) tuples. Where: shaped is the tensor whose shape
to check; tensor_spec is the specification to check it against (see "Shape
specification" in our User Guide); and context contains (optional) information about
where shaped came from - for better error messages.
```

methods.name: check_shapes.checker.ShapeChecker._parse_checks
documentation: Sanity check, register and parse the given `checks` into {class}`_ShapeCheck` objects.

methods.name: check_shapes.checker.ShapeChecker._match_dims
documentation:
Match expected dimensions against actual dimensions.

If some dimensions cannot be determined, the remaining dimensions are added to
`_VariableState.waiting_for_varrank`.

methods.name: check_shapes.checker.ShapeChecker._check_dim
documentation:
Checks that `actual_dim` matches `expected`.

Newly learned information may enable the evaluation of deferred shape checks - any such will
be added to `shape_checks`.

methods.name: check_shapes.checker.ShapeChecker._assert
documentation: Raise a nicely formatted {class}`ShapeMismatchError` if `condition` is not `True`.

Mechanism for checking the shapes of tensors.

This remembers observed shapes and specifications, so that tensors can be checked for
compatibility across multiple calls, and so that we can provide good error messages.

Example:

```{literalinclude} /examples/test_check_shapes_examples.py
:dedent: true
:end-before: '[shape_checker__raw]'
:start-after: '[shape_checker__raw]'
```

functions.name: check_shapes.parser._tree_children
documentation: Return all the children of `tree` that are trees themselves.

functions.name: check_shapes.parser._token_children
documentation: Return the values of all the children of `tree` that are tokens.

functions.name: check_shapes.parser.parse_tensor_spec
documentation: Parse a `check_shapes` tensor specification.

functions.name: check_shapes.parser.parse_function_spec
documentation: Parse all `check_shapes` argument or note specification for a single function.

functions.name: check_shapes.parser.parse_and_rewrite_docstring
documentation: Rewrite `docstring` to include the shapes specified by the `argument_specs`.

classes.name: check_shapes.parser._TreeVisitor
documentation:
Functionality for visiting the nodes of parse-trees.

This differs from the classes built-in in Lark, in that it allows passing `*args` and
`**kwargs`.

Subclasses should add methods with the same name as Lark rules. Those methods should take the
parse tree of the rule, followed by any other `*args` and `**kwargs` you want. They may return
anything.

methods.name: check_shapes.parser._RewritedocString._guess_indent
documentation:
Infer the level of indentation of a docstring.

Returns `None` if the indentation could not be inferred.

classes.name: check_shapes.parser._CachedParser
documentation:
Small wrapper around Lark so that we can reuse as much code as possible between the different
things we parse.
